page: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 2 vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core and 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 480 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and includes 118 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2268.08, and single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 30.79 GB/sec. However, multi-core CPU performance is weak, scoring 2300.17 in stress-ng, which places it in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck; read-only small block cached bandwidth is weak at 164.07 GB/sec, large block uncached read bandwidth is weak at 25.26 GB/sec, and large block uncached write bandwidth is poor, falling into the bottom 10% at 11.86 GB/sec. Other metrics, such as PassMark CPU and Redis SET operations, also fall into the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ds_v7 offers a low-density resource profile suitable for workloads that require high single-threaded clock speeds but do not demand high memory throughput or multi-core scaling. The inclusion of 118 GB of local storage provides value for applications requiring fast local scratch space. This server is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded file compression tasks, and development or testing environments that do not experience heavy concurrent database operations or memory-intensive processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 118 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU profile, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are limited, with uncached write bandwidth falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for entry-level workloads that benefit from high clock speeds but do not require intensive multi-threaded scaling or high memory throughput. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and basic development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance alongside limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v7 is a 2-vCPU general-purpose VM with 8 GB RAM, 118 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dual-vCPU virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds, integrated local storage, and balanced memory for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Includes 118 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Exhibits lower multi-core scaling and uncached memory write bandwidth
Suited for lightweight web serving, development tasks, and single-threaded workloads
